channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Jack Flaherty will start World Series Game 1 for Dodgers
item.description: Flaherty shut out the Mets for seven innings in Game 1 of the NLCS but gave up eight runs in three innings in Game 5. Yoshinobu Yamamoto will start Game 2 against the Yankees.
item.dc:creator: Bill Plunkett, The Mercury News

item.title: Yankees’ Nestor Cortes Jr, dealing with elbow ailment, willing to risk further injury to pitch in World Series
item.description: Nestor Cortes Jr. expects to be on the New York Yankees' World Series roster despite facing the risk of a long-term injury as he is dealing with an elbow ailment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yankees-Dodgers iconic moments: Jackie Robinson steals home in World Series
item.description: One of the most iconic moments in World Series history came in 1955, when Jackie Robinson stole home off Whitey Ford and beat the tag from Yogi Berra to score.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Monday takeaways: Kingsbury versus Waldron, injury concerns and breaking down TJ's Peanut Punch
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. (WGN) — Whether it was more Caleb Williams, the Kliff Kingsbury connection, or TJ Edwards breaking down his rendition of the Peanut Punch, the Chicago Bears were back at Halas Hall Monday for walkthroughs ahead of their primetime matchup against the Washington Commanders.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: After bye week, Bears hope to maintain momentum for upcoming key games
item.description: Given a week off after their offense got rolling against Jacksonville in London, Caleb Williams and the Chicago Bears might have been better served without a Week 7 bye.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ticker-tape parade after NY Liberty win: Everything you need to know
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – After a massive win for the New York Liberty Sunday night, the city is ready to celebrate. On Thursday, New York City will host a ticker-tape parade for the team's first-ever WNBA championship title, Mayor Eric Adams announced. The parade will kick off at 10 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: USU gives up 21 points in fourth quarter, lose to New Mexico
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Utah State's losing streak hit six games, and this one was the most hearbreaking. Devon Dampier and Eli Sanders had touchdown runs in the final 3:53 to rally New Mexico to a 50-45 victory over Utah State on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Grand Junction football cruises to third straight league win
item.description: Entering Thursday with their best start (6-1) since 2011, Grand Junction welcomed 4A I-25 league foe Centaurus.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Shohei Ohtani homers as the Dodgers rout the Mets 8-0 for a 2-1 lead in the NLCS
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — By the time Shohei Ohtani stepped to the plate in the eighth inning Wednesday night, the Los Angeles Dodgers were already in control. Once the ball left his bat, Game 3 of the National League Championship Series was over.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Lonzo Ball makes long-awaited return from knee injuries, scores 10 in Bulls' preseason game
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — When the moment finally arrived, after multiple knee surgeries and one grueling rehab, Lonzo Ball raised his right arm and blew a kiss to the cheering United Center crowd. Fans rose to their feet as the announcement echoed through the arena: “Checking in for the Chicago Bulls! No.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Virginia McCaskey named Top 25 candidate in "Contributor" category for Pro Football HoF's Class of 2025
item.description: CANTON, Ohio (WGN) — Virginia McCaskey has been named one of 25 candidates for the "Contributor" category in the Pro Football Hall of Fame's Class of 2025.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Cole Kmet earns NFC Special Teams Player of the Week honors
item.description: Cole Kmet was named NFC Special Teams Player of the Week for Week 6 after successfully snapping on all five of the Bears' point-after attempts during their game in London against the Jacksonville Jaguars.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
